mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a robust economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon as investors price in future prosperity.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth correlates with corporate profits
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth rate is positive but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals improving labor market health, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Lower unemployment typically associated with economic expansion
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 8.0% and the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is low and the federal funds rate is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment that reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as risk assets are favored.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and low inflation support equity valuations
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2% but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts after a period of decline signals renewed consumer and business confidence in the economy, indicating a bottoming out of a downturn and driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by more than 10% MoM during a recovery regime, but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate strong earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS MoM growth exceeds 0.5% but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling a healthy supply side of the economy that supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO year-over-year growth is positive but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, leading to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon as market participants react to the news.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Strong job growth is a positive economic indicator
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 MoM but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to monetary tightening
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3.0% but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
